lenses.description: The ultra-wide of 7Artisans' affordable Spectrum full-frame cine set, an entry-level manual prime for sweeping landscapes, architecture and tight interiors that, unusually for its width, still accepts front filters. Like the rest of the line it holds a consistent look and gear position across the set for fast changes, with well-controlled focus breathing and an all-metal barrel.

A wide-normal prime in 7Artisans' budget Spectrum full-frame cine line, a practical everyday length for narrative and documentary work. It shares the set's consistent look, unified gear positions and controlled focus breathing in an affordable, all-metal manual body.

The normal-perspective standard in 7Artisans' affordable Spectrum full-frame cine set, a neutral workhorse length for coverage and dialogue. It matches the rest of the line in look, gear position and controlled breathing, an accessible entry into full-frame cine primes.

The portrait-length short telephoto of 7Artisans' Spectrum full-frame cine line, flattering for faces and close detail with smooth subject separation. It keeps the set's consistent look, unified gearing and controlled focus breathing in an affordable manual build.

lenses.description: The wide zoom in Angénieux's Academy Award-winning lightweight Optimo trio, a Super 35 zoom holding a constant T2.6 with no ramping. Under 2kg, it carries the Angénieux look of high contrast and resolution with an organic rendering, and its internal focus design keeps breathing negligible while staying parfocal across the range. Focus travels 320 degrees for precise pulls, and it shares a 114mm front and matched center of gravity with the 28-76 and 45-120 for fast swaps. Appeared in 2006 and remains a shoulder, Steadicam and gimbal staple.

lenses.description: A wide-angle Super 35 studio zoom, a fast constant T2.2 across a 4.7x range with no ramping. Introduced in 2004, it covers the span of the most commonly used primes in a single lens and, at around 5.5kg, is far more manageable than the 24-290, which made it a quick favorite for narrative work and an ideal lens for crane shots. It carries the Angénieux look with excellent optical performance and minimal breathing, on a PL mount easily swapped to Panavision or Canon EF.

lenses.description: An enhanced version of the Optimo 17-80, a Super 35 zoom with a larger image circle and longer reach, holding a constant T2.6 with no ramping and minimal breathing. Its 4.8x range and prime-level speed let it be treated as a variable prime, with the homogeneity of color, contrast and resolution the Optimo line is known for. Introduced in 2012 alongside the 28-340, it is an ideal companion to the industry-reference 12x zooms, on a PL mount interchangeable to Panavision or Canon EF.

lenses.description: The original Optimo 12x, launched in 2001 and so popular on Los Angeles sets that it became known as the Hollywood lens. This Super 35 zoom holds a constant T2.8 across a 12x range with exceptional optical performance and very limited breathing, letting it not only zoom but replace a whole set of primes. It carries the full Angénieux look and remains a reference for feature, commercial and TV work. At around 11kg it is a studio and crane lens rather than a handheld one.

lenses.description: A long-range Super 35 telephoto zoom covering a 12x range at a constant T3.2, with an optical design that eliminates ramping and breathing across the throw. Its reach and prime-rivalling optical quality let it be treated as a variable prime for tighter coverage, and the interchangeable rear assembly converts it toward larger formats while keeping the same physical size. Introduced in 2012 with a 162mm front for larger matte boxes, it is a heavyweight studio and long-lens tool at around 11kg.

Under 2kg with 320 degrees of focus rotation and a 114mm front, it matches the 15-40 and 45-120 for quick interchange on set. Introduced in 2007 and a longtime favorite for narrative and TV work.

Just over 4 pounds with 320 degrees of focus travel and a 114mm front, it intercuts cleanly with the 15-40 and 28-76. Added to the lightweight range in 2011.

lenses.description: A wide Super 35 zoom from Angénieux's Optimo DP series, sometimes called Rouge, holding a constant T2.8. The DP zooms carried the Angénieux look in a compact digital-production body, but their protruding rear elements ruled out use on cameras with spinning mirror shutters, and the series was later discontinued and effectively replaced by the Optimo Style line. On a PL mount, it remains a fast, compact wide zoom for digital cameras that clear its rear elements.

Like its 16-42 sibling it delivered the Angénieux look in a compact digital-production package, but its protruding rear elements prevented use with spinning mirror shutters, and the DP series was later discontinued in favor of the Optimo Style zooms. The Style line refreshed the classic lightweight Optimos for modern digital and mirror-shutter cameras, with a larger image circle up to 34.6mm that covers sensors such as the RED 8K Helium and ARRI Open Gate. lenses.description: The long-range zoom in Angénieux's Optimo Style family, a Super 35 10x zoom holding a constant T3.5 with no ramping. It descends from the classic 25-250 that many cinematographers consider an all-time favorite, and carries the Angénieux look with a large image circle for modern digital cameras. Heavier and longer than the lightweight Style siblings, it is the family's do-everything studio and field zoom, on a PL mount interchangeable to Canon EF or Panavision. Its rear group can also be swapped with the 44-440 anamorphic zoom.

lenses.description: The current-generation Optimo 12x, the modular successor to the classic 24-290, built on Angénieux's Interchangeable Rear Optics so a single lens covers multiple formats. In its Super 35 configuration it is a 24-290mm holding a constant T2.8; a user-swappable rear group converts it to a 36-435mm T4.2 for Full Frame and VistaVision. It keeps the Angénieux look with the homogeneity and very limited breathing of the reference 12x, and anchors the high-end Optimo full-frame collection alongside the Ultra Compact zooms and Optimo primes.

Compact and affordable for a cine zoom, it gives high resolution and contrast, with a Fujinon EBC multi-layer coating that keeps flare and veiling glare down. Breathing is minimal, especially at the wide end, and a nine-blade iris renders rounded, organic bokeh. Its colour and handling are matched to ARRI's prime lenses, on the PL mount with the integrated Lens Data System.

lenses.description: A wide-angle Super 35 studio zoom in the ARRI/Fujinon Alura line, holding a constant T2.6 across the range and the wider of the two Alura studio zooms. It pairs high resolution and contrast with even illumination and a Fujinon EBC coating that suppresses flare, while extraordinary-dispersion glass keeps chromatic aberration low. Breathing is minimal, especially at the wide end, and the nine-blade iris gives rounded bokeh. Colour and handling are matched to ARRI's primes, on the PL mount with LDS, and it complements the longer 45-250mm.

lenses.description: A lightweight standard-range Super 35 zoom in the ARRI/Fujinon Alura line, holding a constant T2.8 across the range. Small and light for quick handling on set, it shares the line's high resolution and contrast, EBC coating and reduced chromatic aberration. Breathing is minimal, and the nine-blade iris renders rounded, organic bokeh. Its colour and handling match ARRI's primes, on the PL mount with the integrated Lens Data System.

lenses.description: A telephoto Super 35 studio zoom in the ARRI/Fujinon Alura line, holding a constant T2.6 across its whole range with no ramping, and the longer of the two Alura studio zooms. It gives high resolution and contrast with an EBC multi-layer coating against flare, and keeps breathing minimal even at the long end. The nine-blade iris renders rounded bokeh, and colour and handling are matched to ARRI's primes on the PL mount with LDS. It complements the wider 18-80mm.

lenses.description: The widest lens in ARRI and Zeiss's Master Prime set, a Super 35 prime opening to the line's very fast constant T1.3. It renders the set's clean, high-contrast image with Zeiss colour and skin tones, holding distortion low even at this extreme angle thanks to aspherical elements. Dual floating elements keep focus breathing virtually absent, and a nine-blade iris gives rounded bokeh. It carries the PL mount with the integrated Lens Data System, matched in colour and ring positions to the rest of the Master Primes.

The PL mount carries LDS metadata, and colour and ring positions match the rest of the set.

lenses.description: The ARRI/Zeiss Master Zoom, a Super 35 zoom covering a 6.6x range at a constant T2.6 with no ramping, built to match the Master Primes. Its prime-like sharpness, contrast and Zeiss skin tones come close to the primes themselves, and cam-style mechanics keep focus breathing effectively absent with almost no geometric distortion across the range. A radical aspherical front element and the T*XP coating hold aberration and veiling glare down. Originally an ARRI Rental exclusive, it is PL-mounted with integrated LDS and color-matched to the rest of the ARRI/Zeiss set.

lenses.description: A telephoto full-frame prime in ARRI's Signature Prime line, still opening to a fast T1.8. It renders the warm Signature look with creamy bokeh and a smooth focus fall-off that suits tight portraits and isolation. Breathing and distortion stay low even at this length, and colour and handling match the rest of the set. It uses the LPL mount with LDS-2 metadata and the shared magnetic rear filter holder.

lenses.description: The widest lens in ARRI's Signature Prime line, a full-frame large-format prime that opens to a fast T1.8. Even at this extreme wide angle it holds distortion remarkably low and keeps focus breathing minimal, in keeping with the set's clean but organic rendering. It carries the warm skin tones, creamy bokeh and smooth focus fall-off shared across the Signatures, on the LPL mount with LDS-2 metadata. The lightweight magnesium housing and the magnetic rear filter holder let you drop in nets or diffusion without breaking the look.

lenses.description: A very wide full-frame zoom in the ARRI Signature line, holding a constant T2.8 from wide to long with no ramping. Built to match the Signature Primes, it carries the same organic, gently softened large-format rendering, with natural skin tones, smooth focus fall-off and rounded bokeh, so it intercuts seamlessly with the primes. Its optical design is unusually sharp for a wide zoom while keeping distortion and breathing close to nothing even at the wide end. Compact and light for its coverage, it sits on the LPL mount with the LDS-2 lens data system and covers sensors from Super 35 up to large format.

It is the versatile mid-range choice, covering most everyday shooting needs, and shares its front diameter with the Signature Primes. Like the rest of the set it renders the organic Signature look with natural skin tones and rounded bokeh, stays close to distortion-free and breathes little, so it cuts together cleanly with the primes. On the LPL mount with the LDS-2 lens data system, it covers Super 35 up to large format.

Despite the reach it stays relatively light for its class and keeps the organic Signature look, with natural skin tones, rounded bokeh and low breathing, so it intercuts with the primes and the shorter zooms. A dedicated 1.7x extender, shared with the 280mm Signature Prime, pushes its reach further while preserving the same character. On the LPL mount with the LDS-2 lens data system, it covers Super 35 up to large format.

lenses.description: A long-telephoto Super 35 prime in the ARRI/Zeiss Ultra Prime set, fast at T1.9. Uniquely in the set it uses a ten-blade iris rather than the usual nine, giving especially round bokeh with a distinct focus fall-off, well suited to portraits and isolation. Its sharp, contrasty rendering and Zeiss skin tones match the rest of the set, with minimal breathing through focus pulls. It is PL-mounted and color-matched across the set.

lenses.description: The ARRI/Zeiss Ultra Wide Zoom, a super-wide Super 35 zoom holding a constant T2.9, and the first super-wide zoom made for professional cine work. A near-telecentric design gives extremely low distortion, so straight lines stay straight even at close focus, with uniform illumination and a fixed entrance pupil across the whole range. It can stand in for a set of wide-angle primes and suits plate shots and other VFX work. It covers the ARRI Open Gate area with room to spare, on the PL mount with integrated LDS and color-matched to the rest of the ARRI/Zeiss lenses.

lenses.description: A 10x CINE-SERVO telephoto covering 25-250mm, aimed at documentary, wildlife and broadcast work. The aperture holds T2.95 from 25 to 187mm and ramps to T3.95 at 250mm, and a built-in 1.5x extender pushes the reach to 375mm at T4.4-5.9. It stays compact and light for its range, with 4K Super 35 coverage, an eleven-blade iris and a detachable servo drive. It slots between the 17-120 and the 50-1000 in the CINE-SERVO family.

fieldNotes.hasFocusBreathing: Significant focus breathing at long focal lengths (850mm and up); the optical design favors a compact form.

lenses.description: An ultra-telephoto 20x CINE-SERVO reaching 1000mm, or 1500mm with its built-in 1.5x extender, long a staple for sports, wildlife and long-range field production. The aperture holds T5.0 from 50 to 560mm and ramps to T8.9 at 1000mm. Fluorite elements control chromatic aberration across the reach, with an eleven-blade iris and a detachable servo drive for broadcast or cine use. It covers Super 35 at 4K and is notably compact and light for its class.

lenses.description: The 30x successor to the 50-1000, both wider and longer at nearly the same size and weight, reaching 1800mm with its built-in 1.5x extender. The aperture holds T5.0 to 560mm and ramps to T10.8 at 1200mm. It covers Super 35 natively and full frame with the extender, with 8K-grade optics, an eleven-blade iris and focus-breathing compensation. Built for wildlife, stadium sports and long-range live production, with a removable e-Xs servo drive for broadcast or cine control.

lenses.description: The 30x successor to the 50-1000, both wider and longer at nearly the same size and weight, reaching 1800mm with its built-in 1.5x extender. The aperture holds T5.0 to 560mm and ramps to T10.8 at 1200mm. It covers Super 35 natively and full frame with the extender, with 8K-grade optics, an eleven-blade iris and focus-breathing compensation. The native RF mount adds distortion correction and real-time metadata for virtual production, driven by a removable e-Xs servo unit for broadcast or cine control.

lenses.description: The widest zoom in Canon's CINE-SERVO line, an ultra-wide 5x reaching about a hundred-degree angle of view at 11mm on Super 35. It is built as an ENG-style servo zoom with a removable e-Xs drive for zoom, focus and iris, and also runs manual for cine setups, with Cooke /i and ZEISS eXtended Data on the PL mount. The aperture ramps gently from T2.95 at the wide end to T3.95 at the long end, and a built-in 1.5x extender both increases reach and enables full-frame coverage over the native Super 35 image circle. An eleven-blade iris, 8K-grade optics and optical focus-breathing correction round it out, and it is the lightest lens in the CINE-SERVO family.

lenses.description: The original CINE-SERVO, a 7x zoom that pairs broadcast ENG handling with cinema optics on Super 35. The aperture holds T2.95 from 17 to 91mm and then ramps to T3.9 at the 120mm end. A detachable servo drive controls zoom, focus and iris, with angle-of-view shift suppression to keep breathing low and an eleven-blade iris for rounded bokeh. It bridges shoulder-mounted broadcast operation and cine-style use at 4K over a Super 35 image circle.

sources.isParfocal: Consistent with CN7x17 KAS-S; parfocal confirmed for series (tier-2)

lenses.description: The 17-120mm CINE-SERVO in its later KAS T generation, sharing the original's optics while adding an enhanced e-Xs servo drive, a native RF option and metadata output for virtual production. The aperture holds T2.95 from 17 to 91mm and then ramps to T3.9 at 120mm. A detachable servo drive controls zoom, focus and iris, with angle-of-view shift suppression to keep breathing low and an eleven-blade iris for rounded bokeh. It covers Super 35 at 4K and bridges broadcast and cine-style operation.

lenses.description: An 8x CINE-SERVO that widens the 17-120 concept to 15mm and adds a built-in 1.5x extender. The aperture holds T2.95 from 15 to 90mm and ramps to T3.95 at 120mm, while engaging the extender shifts the range to 22.5-180mm at T4.4-5.9 and enables full-frame coverage over the native Super 35 circle. It was Canon's first CINE-SERVO to support Dual Pixel autofocus on EF, with 8K-grade optics, reduced breathing and an eleven-blade iris. A detachable servo drive handles zoom, focus and iris for broadcast or cine operation.

lenses.description: The longest prime in Canon's CN-E Cinema Prime set, a full-frame telephoto for tight portraits and detail with strong subject isolation. It renders with Canon's clean, sharp look and rounded bokeh from an eleven-blade iris, holding edge-to-edge brightness across the frame. Manual focus with a smooth stepless throw and a shared front diameter, gear positions and form factor for fast, matched swaps. It covers full-frame and Super 35 to 4K-grade standards and colour-matches the CN-E primes.

lenses.description: A wide-angle Super 35 cinema zoom with a constant T2.8 across its 3x range, matched to the 30-105mm T2.8 as a lightweight two-lens set. Fluorite and large aspherical elements keep it sharp and hold breathing to near zero, and it is parfocal, maintaining focus through the zoom without centre-axis drift. An eleven-blade iris gives smooth rounded bokeh, and the light 4K-grade build suits handheld and documentary work. It shares a 114mm front and 105mm filter thread with its 30-105 sibling.

lenses.description: A compact, lightweight Super 35 servo zoom with a constant T4.4 across its range, one half of Canon's Compact-Servo pair with the 70-200mm T4.4. It was Canon's first cine-style lens with image stabilization and autofocus support on Cinema EOS bodies, and it is parfocal in both manual and autofocus modes. A nine-blade iris gives rounded bokeh, and a detachable servo grip or standard controllers drive zoom, focus and iris, making it well suited to run-and-gun, live and remote use. It threads for 77mm screw-in filters behind a removable sunshade.

lenses.description: One of Canon's first full-frame cinema zooms, a constant-T2.4 large-format zoom in the Flex Zoom series. The T-stop stays constant across the range, with an eleven-blade iris, 8K-grade optics and the warm, colour-matched Cinema EOS look, and it is parfocal, holding focus through the zoom. It pairs with the 45-135mm T2.4 as a matched full-frame set, sharing front diameter and gears for fast swaps. The Flex design also allows relay-kit reconfiguration between full-frame and Super 35 coverage.

lenses.description: The telephoto half of Canon's Compact-Servo pair, a light Super 35 servo zoom with a constant T4.4 matched to the 18-80mm T4.4. It carries image stabilization and autofocus support on Cinema EOS bodies and is a true parfocal design that holds focus through the zoom. A nine-blade iris gives rounded bokeh, and small side buttons plus standard controllers or the optional grip handle the servo zoom for live, jib and remote setups. It shares the compact form, 77mm filter thread and handling of its 18-80 sibling.

lenses.description: A fast portrait-length prime in Canon's CN-E Cinema Prime set, well suited to close-ups and strong subject separation. Its bright aperture delivers shallow depth of field and low-light headroom, with Canon's clean, sharp look and smooth rounded bokeh from an eleven-blade iris. Manual focus with a smooth stepless throw; shared front diameter, gear positions and form factor let it swap cleanly with the CN-E 24 and 50 and the rest of the set. Full-frame and Super 35 coverage at 4K-grade sharpness, colour-matched across the set.

lenses.description: A long-serving fast short telephoto, a favourite for interviews and compression on full-frame cine rigs. The f/2 aperture isolates subjects cleanly; it has no IS and photo-lens breathing, but its speed and rendering keep it popular decades on.

lenses.description: Canon's fast constant-aperture ultra-wide L zoom, a staple run-and-gun wide on EF cinema bodies and adapted mirrorless rigs. The f/2.8 aperture and full-frame coverage make it a practical single-lens wide for documentary and event work where swapping primes is not an option. No stabilization, and some edge softness and distortion are the trade for speed and range.

lenses.description: The image-stabilized f/4 sibling to the fast 16-35, trading a stop for IS and cleaner corners. On a gimbal or handheld cine rig the stabilization steadies wide handheld moves while the constant f/4 keeps exposure flat through the zoom. A common documentary and B-roll wide on Canon cinema bodies.

lenses.description: A wide-to-normal Super 35 zoom in the Cooke S4/i family, holding a constant fast T-stop across the range through a variable vignetting stop that prevents ramping. It carries the warm Cooke Look with smooth bokeh and negligible focus breathing, plus an unusually close focus, matching the character of the S4/i primes. /i Technology metadata and color matching let it intercut seamlessly with an S4/i set.

lenses.description: A moderate wide full-frame prime in the S7/i set, a flexible focal length for coverage and moving-camera work on large-format cameras. It carries the trademark Cooke warmth, smooth tonal roll-off and rounded creamy bokeh, with organic, well-managed flare. Its generous image circle, /i metadata and color match make it intercuttable across full-frame Cooke sets.

lenses.description: A near-normal full-frame prime in the S7/i Full Frame Plus set, close to natural perspective for dialogue and general coverage. The Cooke Look gives it gentle contrast, natural color and soft rounded bokeh, with minimal breathing on the long focus throw. Color-matched and /i-enabled, it covers full frame and beyond for large-format work.

The large image circle, /i metadata and color matching keep it consistent with the rest of the line.

Its rendering carries the warm Cooke Look, smooth focus falloff and rounded creamy bokeh, with minimal breathing. /i Technology metadata and color matching make it accurate to pull and intercuttable across the range.

lenses.description: A portrait-length full-frame prime in the S7/i set, suited to close-ups and separating a subject from its background on large formats. The Cooke Look brings warm skin tones, gentle contrast and soft creamy bokeh, with a long smooth focus throw and minimal breathing. Its generous image circle, /i metadata and color match keep it consistent across full-frame Cooke sets.

A telephoto full-frame prime in Cooke's S8/i set, favoured for close-ups and subject separation. Opening wide for a razor-thin depth of field and low-light work, it carries the warm Cooke Look with creamy rounded bokeh and minimal colour fringing from its all-spherical design. Optimised contrast and gentle falloff give an organic, dimensional image. Consistent front diameter and gears, /i Technology and Cooke colour matching keep it intercuttable across the S8/i primes.

The longest prime in our S8/i Full Frame Plus set, a long telephoto for tight portraits and detail with strong subject isolation. It opens wide for a razor-thin depth of field, rendering the warm Cooke Look with creamy bokeh, flattering skin tones and minimal fringing from its all-spherical optics. Controlled contrast and smooth falloff keep the image organic and filmic. Shared front diameter and gear positions, /i Technology metadata and colour matching keep it consistent with the rest of the S8/i set.

The widest prime in Cooke's S8/i Full Frame Plus set, a fast wide-angle for establishing shots and interiors on full-frame sensors. As the fastest of Cooke's spherical full-frame primes, it opens right up for an extra stop in low light while holding the warm, organic Cooke Look. The all-spherical design gives clean, smooth bokeh and minimal colour fringing with a strong sense of depth. A consistent front diameter and gear positions across the set, /i Technology metadata and Cooke colour matching let it swap and intercut cleanly with the rest of the S8/i primes.

A wide full-frame prime in Cooke's S8/i set, a flexible focal length for landscapes, interiors and wider coverage. It carries the warm Cooke Look with an all-spherical rendering, clean smooth bokeh and minimal fringing, opening wide for an extra stop in dim conditions. Optimised contrast and gentle falloff give an organic, filmic feel rather than a clinical one. Shared front diameter and gear positions, /i Technology metadata and colour matching keep it intercuttable across the S8/i set.

A wide-to-normal full-frame prime in Cooke's S8/i set, close to a versatile everyday angle of view. It renders with the warm Cooke Look, flattering skin tones and smooth out-of-focus falloff from an all-spherical design, opening wide for low-light work. Clean bokeh and minimal colour fringing keep the image organic and dimensional. Consistent front diameter and gears, /i Technology and colour matching let it slot seamlessly into an S8/i set.

A normal-lens full-frame prime in Cooke's S8/i set, a natural-perspective focal length for general coverage. It delivers the warm Cooke Look with smooth rounded bokeh and minimal fringing from its all-spherical optics, opening wide for an extra stop of exposure. Optimised contrast gives a filmic, dimensional image with flattering skin tones. Shared front diameter and gear positions, /i Technology metadata and Cooke colour matching make it intercut cleanly with the rest of the set.

A standard full-frame prime in Cooke's S8/i set, a staple focal length near natural perspective. It renders with the warm, organic Cooke Look, flattering skin tones and creamy rounded bokeh, opening wide for shallow depth of field and low light. The all-spherical design keeps colour fringing minimal and contrast controlled for a filmic feel. Consistent front diameter and gears, /i Technology and colour matching keep it intercuttable across the S8/i primes.

A portrait-length full-frame prime in Cooke's S8/i set, well suited to close-ups with a warm, long-lens feel. It gives smooth focus falloff and creamy bokeh from its all-spherical optics, opening wide for shallow depth of field and an extra stop in dim conditions. Flattering skin tones and controlled contrast keep the look organic rather than clinical. Shared front diameter and gear positions, /i Technology metadata and colour matching let it intercut cleanly across the set.

lenses.description: The longest prime in Cooke's SP3 full-frame set, a compact telephoto for tight portraits and detail with pleasant subject isolation. It carries the classic Cooke Look from the Speed Panchro design, warm and dimensional with gentle edge fall-off and smooth nine-blade bokeh. Slightly larger at the front than the rest of the series, it stays color-matched to the SP3 primes and to Panchro/i Classic FF for consistent rendering.

lenses.description: The widest prime in Cooke's SP3 full-frame set, a compact wide-angle for establishing shots, interiors and handheld or gimbal work. Based on the classic Speed Panchro design, it carries the warm Cooke Look with a gentle fall-off toward the frame edge, flattering rendering and a nine-bladed iris for smooth out-of-focus areas. Color-matched across the SP3 series and to the Panchro/i Classic FF primes, with user-changeable mirrorless mounts, it suits a lightweight B or C camera alongside a larger Cooke set.

lenses.description: A near-normal full-frame prime in the SP3 set, a documentary and interview favourite for its compact size and natural perspective. The Speed Panchro-based design gives it the warm Cooke Look, flattering skin tones and a gentle roll-off toward the edges, with a nine-bladed iris for pleasing out-of-focus rendering. It is color-matched across the SP3 series and to Panchro/i Classic FF, with user-changeable mirrorless mounts.

lenses.description: A standard full-frame prime in the SP3 set, a natural-perspective focal length for narrative and handheld coverage on mirrorless cameras. It renders with the classic Cooke Look from the Speed Panchro lineage: warm and dimensional, with gentle edge fall-off and smooth nine-blade bokeh. Compact and color-matched to the SP3 series and Panchro/i Classic FF, it makes a light B or C camera companion.

lenses.description: A classic Super 35 cine zoom from Cooke, spanning wide to telephoto with the vintage Cooke Look. It renders with lower contrast, gently lifted blacks and an organic, filmic softness that avoids a clinical feel, with warm, flattering skin tones and smooth focus falloff. Parfocal with an unusually close focus, it holds its aperture across the range and remains a versatile single-lens workhorse.

lenses.description: The long-range classic Super 35 cine zoom in Cooke's vintage Varotal family, a 10:1 range reaching from wide to a long telephoto. It carries the warm vintage Cooke Look with softness and expressive roll-off, giving elegant compression for portraits and long-lens work. Parfocal and holding its aperture across the range, it delivers a textured, filmic image rather than a clinical modern one.

lenses.description: The wide-angle zoom in Cooke's modern Varotal/i FF full-frame set, holding a constant T-stop across the range as a zoomable prime. Matched in resolution, color and fall-off to the S7/i and S8/i FF primes, it renders the warm Cooke Look with natural, gentle skin tones and minimal focus breathing through a nine-blade iris. /i Technology metadata, including calibrated shading and distortion maps, keeps it intercuttable across a full-frame Cooke set.

lenses.description: A telephoto large-format prime in the Arles set, well suited to portraits, interviews and tighter coverage. It carries the line's clean, modern sharpness and gentle, painterly feel with natural colour and fine skin rendering, a sixteen-blade iris for smooth round bokeh and floating elements holding breathing low. Wide open it softens into a lower-contrast look with shallow, dimensional separation, in the set's consistent housing and gearing.

lenses.description: A telephoto large-format prime in the Arles set, a longer portrait and detail lens with an arrestingly shallow depth of field wide open. It renders with the Arles balance of clean, modern sharpness and a gentle, painterly character, natural colour and a sixteen-blade iris for smooth round bokeh, with floating elements keeping breathing minimal. Opened up it eases into a softer, low-contrast rendering, sharing the set's front and gearing for fast changes.

lenses.description: The widest lens in DZOFilm's flagship Arles Prime set, an ultra-wide large-format prime that opens up sweeping vistas and tight interiors while keeping wide-angle distortion well controlled. It carries the Arles signature: a clean, modern sharpness paired with a gentle, painterly rendering, natural colour and a sixteen-blade iris for round out-of-focus highlights, with floating elements holding breathing to a minimum, and a nano coating keeping flare and ghosting in check. Its front takes no screw-in filter thread, so it works with a matte box rather than screw-in filters.

lenses.description: The wide member of DZOFilm's full-frame Catta Ace zoom trio, a compact, lightweight cine zoom covering wide-angle to standard framing for handheld, documentary and run-and-gun work. It renders with natural, true-to-life colour and soft, pleasing skin tones, a smooth focus-to-defocus transition and a rounded iris that turns out-of-focus areas into gentle, dreamy bokeh, with breathing kept close to invisible when pulling focus. A remarkably short close-focus distance lets you move right in on a subject while still holding the wider scene.

lenses.description: The widest lens in DZOFilm's full-frame Gnosis Macro set, a wide-angle prime with true built-in macro capability that lets you get extremely close while still taking in the surrounding scene. It renders with crisp, edge-to-edge sharpness from its close-focus distance out to infinity and a clean, natural colour that stays uniform from in-focus to out-of-focus, with a sixteen-blade iris for smooth, naturally soft bokeh. Because it focuses to infinity as well, it doubles as a wide general-purpose prime, not just a macro.

lenses.description: The widest zoom in DZOFilm's Pictor line, a compact, lightweight Super 35 parfocal cine zoom covering the ultra-wide range. Close focusing suits tight spaces, and distortion stays well controlled even wide open. Focus holds through the zoom, internal floating elements keep breathing very low, and a 16-blade iris renders soft, rounded bokeh. Color and handling are matched across the Pictor set, with standard cine gears and an interchangeable PL/EF mount.

lenses.description: The wide-to-standard half of DZOFilm's Super 35 Tango zoom duo, a parfocal cine zoom built to cross over between cinema and ENG-style shooting: it holds focus right through the zoom, so you can push in mid-shot without re-focusing. It renders with lifelike, natural colour and clean skin tones, a sixteen-blade iris for smooth round bokeh, and a subtle blue flare from its multi-layer coating. Controlled breathing and luminous focus and zoom marks make it a practical choice for documentary, broadcast, events and sports, and it accepts an optional servo for remote focus, iris and zoom.

lenses.description: The wide-to-standard half of DZOFilm's Super 35 Tango zoom duo, a parfocal cine zoom built to cross over between cinema and ENG-style shooting: it holds focus right through the zoom, so you can push in mid-shot without re-focusing. It renders with lifelike, natural colour and clean skin tones, a sixteen-blade iris for smooth round bokeh, and a subtle blue flare from its multi-layer coating. Controlled breathing and luminous focus and zoom marks make it a practical choice for documentary, broadcast, events and sports, and it accepts an optional servo for remote focus, iris and zoom.

lenses.description: The telephoto half of DZOFilm's Super 35 Tango zoom duo, a long-reaching parfocal cine zoom that partners the wider 18-90mm to cover an extensive range in just two lenses. It holds focus through the zoom for push-ins without re-focusing, rendering with lifelike, natural colour, a sixteen-blade iris for smooth round bokeh and a gentle blue flare from its coating. Its long reach compresses perspective and isolates subjects, making it well suited to sports, events, wildlife and documentary, and it takes the same optional servo for ENG-style remote control.

lenses.description: The widest lens in DZOFilm's original Vespid Prime set, an ultra-wide full-frame cine prime that keeps distortion well controlled for its angle of view. It carries the line's modern-vintage balance: an APO design that stays sharp and clean while rendering with a gentle, organic character and neutral colour, with a sixteen-blade iris for round out-of-focus highlights and minimal focus breathing. Lightweight and compact like the rest of the agile-wasp family, it shares the common gear positions that make swaps quick. Its front carries an anti-reflective extinction thread rather than a filter thread, so it works with a matte box rather than screw-in filters.

It keeps the line's modern-vintage balance, an APO design that stays sharp and clean while rendering organically with neutral colour, and a sixteen-blade iris for smooth round bokeh with minimal breathing. lenses.description: The wide-standard lens in DZOFilm's Vespid Cyber set, a full-frame prime that inherits the Vespid Prime optical character and adds a built-in data interface. Its APO design renders with the organic, gently sharp Vespid look and a sixteen-blade iris for round bokeh, while an internal-focus design keeps the front from rotating or changing size and holds breathing low. The Cyber data connection outputs focus and aperture data for VFX and virtual-production workflows and enables autofocus with compatible third-party systems, behind a larger front that matches the rest of the Cyber set.

lenses.description: The longest lens in the second-generation Vespid Prime II set, a full-frame short telephoto with a fast constant T1.9 for portraits and tighter, isolated coverage. Gen 2 retains the Vespid look while upgrading the optics for cleaner, purer imagery with rich detail and natural tonality. Floating elements sharpen close focus, and Cooke /i metadata, a ten-blade iris and a compact all-metal PL body round out a set built for modern, metadata-driven workflows.

lenses.description: The widest lens in the Vespid Retro set, an ultra-wide full-frame prime that pairs a controlled wide-angle rendering with the Retro line's vintage character. It shares the original Vespid optics and mechanics, but a golden coating gives a cool, blue-toned base image with warm, controllable amber flares and a gentler sharpness for a sun-soaked, art-house feel. A sixteen-blade iris keeps bokeh round and breathing minimal, all in the Retro's distinctive silver housing. Its front carries an extinction thread rather than a filter thread, so it works with a matte box rather than screw-in filters.

lenses.description: DZOFilm's full-frame X-Tract probe zoom, a specialist macro lens built to reach into narrow, hidden spaces and pull tiny worlds up to cinematic scale. Its slender, spacecraft-styled barrel slips between book pages or into miniature sets, delivering high-magnification macro with crisp, controlled detail and a creamy bokeh that is especially lovely toward the long end. Being a parfocal zoom, it holds focus while you change framing, opening up push-in and vertigo-style moves at macro range, and its rugged, weather-sealed build stands up to demanding close-up and product work.

lenses.description: The wide zoom of Fujinon's Premier HK Super 35 family, a 3.1x covering 14.5 to 45mm at a constant T2.0. Part of the gold-band 4K+ Premier line, it is matched to its three siblings for focus, iris, zoom, colour and contrast, with minimal breathing and a 280-degree focus rotation. Its speed and edge-to-edge consistency rival primes, aimed at high-end feature and commercial work. It shares the 136mm front and handling of the rest of the HK set for seamless swaps.

lenses.description: The all-purpose Premier HK zoom, a 4.7x holding a constant T2.0 over the most-used 18 to 85mm range on Super 35. A gold-band 4K+ Premier lens, it is colour- and contrast-matched to the other three HK zooms, with minimal breathing and prime-level resolution across the range. It covers the workhorse focal lengths for dramatic studio and location scenes in a single lens, with front diameter, gearing and focus throw matched to the family.

lenses.description: The super-telephoto Premier HK, a 5.3x Super 35 zoom that holds T2.8 from 75 to 270mm and ramps to T3.8 at 400mm, the fastest long reach in the gold-band Premier line. It is built for sports, wildlife, action and compression work where a long, fast lens saves lens changes. It stays colour- and contrast-matched to the shorter HK zooms with a 9-blade iris and minimal breathing. Its speed and reach in a manageable body make it a distinctive tool at the long end.

lenses.description: The general-purpose long member of the Premier HK family, a 7.5x Super 35 zoom at a constant T2.6 across 24 to 180mm. It carries the gold-band Premier line's matched colour, contrast and minimal-breathing behaviour, with prime-grade resolution from centre to corner. Its wide-to-long reach at a fixed T-stop makes it a versatile single-lens option on feature and high-end broadcast sets, sharing the 136mm front and handling of the HK set.

lenses.description: The wide, compact member of Fujinon's Duvo hybrid line, a 7.1x Super 35 zoom that opens to T2.9 from 14mm and holds to 75mm before ramping to T3.9 at 100mm; a built-in 1.5x expander shifts it to 21-150mm for full-frame coverage. It pairs a Premista-style cinematic look with broadcast operation through an ENG-style drive, plus breathing compensation and illumination correction. The smallest and lightest Duvo, aimed at handheld and multi-camera live production.

fieldNotes.hasFocusBreathing: With BCT (Breathing Compensation Technology) enabled: minimal. BCT can be toggled on/off via the lens control system.

lenses.description: The versatile long Duvo, a 12.5x Super 35 zoom that opens to T2.9 and ramps to T4.2 at 300mm, with a built-in 1.5x expander extending it to 36-450mm for full-frame. Effectively the Super 35 equivalent of a 2/3-inch ENG lens, it brings a Fujinon cinematic look to broadcast handling with an ENG-style servo drive, breathing and chromatic-aberration correction, and eXtended Data output. Shoulder-mountable at a manageable weight, it targets sports, live events and documentary and intercuts with the wider Duvo and the Premista and Cabrio families.

lenses.description: The Duvo box lens, a 40x Super 35 super-telephoto that runs from a fast f/2.8 at 25mm, holding to 465mm before ramping to f/5.0 at 1000mm; a built-in 1.5x expander reaches 37.5-1500mm with full-frame coverage. Fujinon rates it in f-stops in keeping with its broadcast box-lens design, and it is the only Duvo with optical image stabilization, using a ceramic-ball roller system against vibration. It adds breathing, illumination and chromatic-aberration correction and works with existing box-lens accessories for multi-camera live production. The longest reach available for large-sensor cine cameras, built for sports and concert broadcast.

lenses.description: The wide-standard MK, an affordable, sub-1kg Super 35 zoom holding a constant T2.9 across 18 to 55mm. Fully manual with geared focus, zoom and iris rings and a clickless iris, it is built for owner-operators on compact cine and mirrorless bodies. It suppresses focus shift and breathing well and is colour-matched to the pricier HK, ZK and XK lenses for mixed sets. A common wide-to-normal choice for handheld and gimbal work, paired with the 50-135mm for a full 18-135mm range.

lenses.description: The telephoto MK, matching its 18-55mm sibling in size, weight and a constant T2.9, covering 50 to 135mm on Super 35. Fully manual with 0.8-pitch gears and a clickless iris, it holds low distortion and controlled breathing at a low price point. Colour-matched to the wider MK and to Fujinon's HK, ZK and XK lines, it handles close-ups and long-lens work in a compact body. Together with the 18-55mm it forms a light, affordable two-lens cine kit.

lenses.description: The wide anchor of Fujifilm's Premista trio, the brand's first large-format cine zooms, holding a constant T2.9 across 19 to 45mm. It covers a large image circle that reaches full-frame and beyond, with a 13-blade iris for rounded bokeh and low distortion into the corners. Focus breathing is well controlled and its colour is matched to the 28-100mm and 80-250mm so the three intercut without a grading shift. A common wide-to-normal companion on large-format cinema and high-end broadcast rigs.

sources.hasFocusBreathing: Fujifilm Fujinon Catalogue 2023 PDF (Tier 1)

lenses.description: The standard-range Premista, holding a constant T2.9 across the full 28 to 100mm span on large-format sensors. The 13-blade iris and controlled breathing give it prime-like rendering through the zoom, replacing a bag of primes on set, with matched colour and consistent contrast from centre to edge. It shares ring positions, front diameter and weight with its 19-45mm and 80-250mm siblings for fast swaps. A workhorse on full-frame cinema and premium broadcast productions.

sources.hasFocusBreathing: Fujifilm Fujinon Catalogue 2023 PDF (Tier 1)
sources.filterType: bhphotovideo.com; abelcine.com; ottonemenz.com -- 114mm front diameter, matte box only
sources.gearPitch: B&H Photo (Tier 2): "Standard 0.8 MOD Gears"

lenses.description: The telephoto Premista, running a constant T2.9 from 80 to 200mm before ramping gently to T3.5 by 250mm, still under a full stop. It carries the same 13-blade iris, large-format coverage and matched colour as the rest of the line, cutting cleanly against the 28-100mm. Breathing stays minimal through the long end and its optical quality rivals primes across the range. A go-to for crane, aerial and long-lens large-format work where lens changes are impractical.

sources.hasFocusBreathing: fujifilm.com XK pagina; bhphotovideo.com reviewer
sources.filterType: tanotis.com; hotrodcameras.com
sources.isParfocal: Fujifilm Optical Devices specifications -- fujifilm.com (Tier 1)

lenses.description: Fujinon's affordable Super 35 Cabrio, a 6x zoom holding a constant T3.5 with no ramp across the full 20 to 120mm range. It covers the Super 35 format with a 9-blade iris and a detachable ENG-style servo grip, doubling as a manual PL cine lens or a run-and-gun broadcast zoom. Colour-matched to the HK, ZK and MK lenses, it trades a slightly slower stop for a wide focal range at a low price. A common single-lens choice for 4K documentary and event work.

lenses.description: The wide-angle member of Fujinon's Super 35 Cabrio family, a 2.5x zoom holding a constant T2.9 across 14 to 35mm. It pairs a cine build with a detachable ENG-style servo grip, switching between broadcast handling and manual cine operation, with a 9-blade iris. Breathing is well controlled and the rendering is colour-matched to the other Cabrios so a mixed set intercuts cleanly. A go-to fast, light wide zoom for documentary and event work.

sources.hasFocusBreathing: Duclos Lenses technical overview -- ducloslenses.com (Tier 2)
sources.gearPitch: B&H Photo (Tier 2): "standard 0.8 film pitch"
sources.isParfocal: Fujifilm ZK Cabrio Series product page -- fujifilm.com (Tier 1)

lenses.description: The workhorse Cabrio, a 4.7x Super 35 zoom at a constant T2.9 over the frequently used 19 to 90mm range. Its detachable servo grip lets it work as a broadcast ENG lens or a manual cine zoom, with a 9-blade iris and low breathing for clean focus pulls. Colour and contrast are matched to the wider 14-35mm and the long Cabrios. Long a default single-lens choice for documentary, sports and small cine crews.

sources.hasFocusBreathing: Film and Digital Times -- fdtimes.com (Tier 3)
sources.filterType: bhphotovideo.com; hotrodcameras.com
sources.gearPitch: Z-Systems + FUJINON.com product pages (Tier 2/1): "0.8mm gear pitch"
sources.isParfocal: Fujifilm ZK Cabrio Series product page -- fujifilm.com (Tier 1)

lenses.description: The long-range Cabrio, a 12x Super 35 zoom that holds T3.5 from 25 to 273mm and loses under half a stop to T3.85 by 300mm, keeping the ramp barely noticeable through a continuous move. It uses the same detachable ENG servo interface and 9-blade iris as the rest of the line, colour-matched so it intercuts with the other Cabrios. Its wide-to-long reach without a lens change suits live events, wildlife and sports where reach and fast handling both matter.

sources.hasFocusBreathing: B&H Photo technical data -- bhphotovideo.com (Tier 2)
sources.gearPitch: New Circuit product page (Tier 3): "standard 0.8mm cine gearing"
sources.isParfocal: Fujifilm ZK Cabrio Series product page -- fujifilm.com (Tier 1)

lenses.description: The telephoto Cabrio, a 3.5x Super 35 zoom that opens to T2.9 from 85 to 215mm and ramps to T4.0 at 300mm. It blends Fujifilm broadcast and cine technology in a lightweight body with a detachable servo drive and a 9-blade iris, colour-matched to the other Cabrios. Controlled breathing keeps long-lens focus work clean. A common fast-telephoto partner to the 19-90mm on documentary and event rigs.

It gives rental crews a single-lens alternative to a bag of ultra-wide primes, with the clean, high-contrast rendering Vantage builds into its spherical glass. Rental-only through Vantage Film.

It stays sharp and on-target through long throws, suited to sport, wildlife and other reach-hungry Super 35 work. Rental-only through Vantage Film.

lenses.description: A large, industry-standard spherical telephoto zoom that keeps its aperture constant across a formidable Super 35 range, engineered for stadium, crowd and other extreme-distance shots. Its long barrel is built for centering stability rather than handheld use, and a supplied reducer converts it to the faster, shorter 100-300mm configuration. Rental-only through Vantage Film.

lenses.description: Laowa's first dedicated cinema zoom, a versatile all-in-one Super 35 lens built to blend vintage and modern character. Colour and bokeh lean vintage, with natural, healthy skin tones and smooth transitions, while its sharpness and a not-overly-contrasty rendering keep it usable on modern productions. Constant T2.9 across the range, parfocal, with low focus breathing and a 9-blade iris, it suits everything from narrative to documentary in one piece of glass.

lenses.description: The widest member of Laowa's full-frame Ranger set, a compact constant-T2.9 zoom that reaches an expansive wide angle while holding exposure and focus across the range. Its clean, modern rendering keeps distortion low and contrast crisp, and the parfocal design with near-invisible breathing lets operators reframe and refocus without hunting. Matched in look, gearing and front size to the rest of the Ranger line for fast swaps on run-and-gun and documentary work.

lenses.description: Laowa's ultra-telephoto broadcast zoom, an industry-leading 24x range that covers sweeping wide shots through extreme long-distance close-ups on a single Super 35 lens. Built for live events, sports and wildlife, it holds a constant aperture across the zoom with true parfocal performance and exceptionally low focus breathing, and its optical design supports 4K and 8K acquisition. A cost-effective route to long-range reach where a full broadcast box lens would otherwise be needed.

lenses.description: The longest lens in the Leitz HEKTOR set, a T2.1 full-frame telephoto for mirrorless cameras, compressing portraits and details with the line's playful, subject-centric character. It renders a slightly softer centre with subtle fall-off toward the edges and a gentle Petzval swirl, in warm natural Leitz tones with colourful flares and contrast held across the frame. The nine-blade iris stays circular at every stop. Fully manual, compact and light, with an 80mm front, 77mm filter thread, matched gears, 120-degree focus rotation and a swappable mirrorless mount.

lenses.description: The widest lens in the Leitz HEKTOR set, a T2.1 full-frame wide for mirrorless cameras, designed and built in Wetzlar by the team behind the Summilux-C and HUGO. Its character-first look draws on a century of Leica and cine optics: a slightly softer centre resolution falling off toward the edges, a gentle Petzval swirl that pulls the eye to the subject, warm natural Leitz tones and colourful flares with contrast held across the frame. The nine-blade iris stays round through every stop. Fully manual and compact, with an 80mm front, matched gears, 120-degree focus rotation and a user-swappable mirrorless mount.

lenses.description: The longest lens in the Leitz HUGO set, a T1.9 full-frame telephoto that brings Leica M reach into a cine housing for compressed portraits and distant subjects. It keeps the HUGO signature of rich colours, artful flares and a rounded image field, with a sharp centre and a gentle fall-off toward the edges that isolates the subject. Sharp without harshness, with lower contrast and a natural roll-off. It shares the set's 95mm front, 0.8 gears, 270-degree focus rotation, improved close focus and swappable LPL or L mount.

lenses.description: The widest lens in the Leitz HUGO set, a T1.5 full-frame wide built around Leica M optics in a true cine housing. It carries the HUGO signature of rich colours, artful flares and a rounded image field, where a gentle fall-off of light and focus toward the corners pulls the eye to the centre of the frame. Wide open it stays sharp without harshness, with lower contrast and a natural fall-off. Compact and light, it suits gimbals, drones and handheld work, with a 95mm front, 0.8 gears, long 270-degree focus rotation and greatly improved close focus over the M 0.8 lenses it succeeds.

lenses.description: A moderate wide T1.5 full-frame prime in the Leitz HUGO set, a classic Leica M focal length in a cine housing, natural for street-flavoured and documentary-style framing. It renders rich colours and artful flares with the rounded image field the line is known for: a sharp centre with a gradual fall-off toward the edges that adds dimensionality. Sharp wide open, never harsh, with lower contrast and smooth roll-off. Compact and light, with the shared 95mm front, 0.8 gears, 270-degree focus rotation and much closer focus than the M 0.8.

lenses.description: A standard-wide T1.5 full-frame prime in the Leitz HUGO set, built on the Leica M 35mm optics that generations of photographers reached for first. In its cine housing it keeps the HUGO character: rich colours, artful flares and a rounded image field with gentle corner fall-off that leads the eye to the subject. Wide open it stays sharp without harshness, with lower contrast and natural fall-off. Compact and light for gimbal and handheld work, with a 95mm front, 0.8 gears, 270-degree focus rotation and greatly improved close focus.

lenses.description: The newest lens in the Leitz HUGO set, a T1.5 full-frame 40mm sitting between the 35 and 50, a focal length cinematographers kept requesting. No 40mm exists in Leica's M line, so this one was designed for HUGO with support from Leica Camera's designers to match the family look: rich colours, artful flares and a rounded image field with a sharp centre and gentle fall-off toward the edges. Wide open it is sharp without harshness, with lower contrast and smooth roll-off. Compact and light, with the shared 95mm front, 0.8 gears and 270-degree focus rotation.

lenses.description: The fastest lens in the Leitz HUGO set, a full-frame 50mm at T1.0 using the glass of Leica's legendary 50mm f/0.95 Noctilux-M. It brings that iconic ultra-shallow rendering into a cine housing: a razor-thin plane of focus, dreamy fall-off and the HUGO signature of rich colours, artful flares and a rounded image field. Even at this extreme speed it stays sharp at the centre without harshness. Compact for what it is, with the set's 95mm front, 0.8 gears, 270-degree focus rotation and swappable LPL or L mount.

It renders the HUGO character of rich colours, artful flares and a rounded image field, with a sharp centre and a gentle fall-off toward the corners that gives faces and subjects a dimensional pop. lenses.description: One of the more unusual lenses in the Leitz HUGO set: a T2.1 full-frame 66mm from the HUGO II line whose optical design revives the fabled Leica ELCAN 'Spy Lens' of the 1960s and 70s, one of the rarest lenses Leica ever produced. It offers a distinctive in-between focal length with the HUGO character of rich colours, artful flares and a rounded image field, in a smaller, lighter housing than the T1.5 lenses. Sharp without harshness, with lower contrast and a natural fall-off. It shares the set's 95mm front, 0.8 gears, 270-degree focus rotation and swappable LPL or L mount.

lenses.description: A short-telephoto T1.5 full-frame prime in the Leitz HUGO set, bringing Leica M portrait optics into a cine housing. It renders the HUGO signature of rich colours, artful flares and a rounded image field, with the corner fall-off in light and focus that lifts a face out of the background. Wide open it stays sharp without harshness, with lower contrast and a smooth, flattering roll-off. Compact and light for its speed, with a 95mm front, 0.8 gears, 270-degree focus rotation and greatly improved close focus over the M 0.8.

lenses.description: A telephoto T1.5 full-frame prime in the Leitz HUGO set, built on Leica M optics in a cine housing and the longest of the T1.5 line's classic portrait lengths. It renders rich colours, artful flares and a rounded image field, with the fall-off toward the corners that isolates a subject and pulls it forward. Wide open it is sharp without harshness, with lower contrast and a smooth, flattering roll-off. With a 95mm front, 0.8 gears, 270-degree focus rotation and greatly improved close focus, it stays compact for a fast telephoto.

It carries the HUGO character of rich colours, artful flares and a rounded image field with gentle corner fall-off, staying sharp without harshness and rolling off naturally. lenses.description: A telephoto T2.0 prime in the Leitz Summicron-C set for Super 35, reaching into tighter compression for portraits, detail and distant subjects. It keeps the set's high resolution and low distortion with telecentric even illumination. Natural, dimensional skin tones and a gentle roll-off keep talent looking natural, with a soft, naturalistic bokeh, colour-matched to intercut with the Summilux-C primes. PL mount, a 95mm front and matched gear positions keep it consistent with the rest of the set.

lenses.description: The widest lens in the Leitz Summicron-C set, a T2.0 ultra-wide for Super 35 that takes in large spaces, tight interiors and dramatic perspective. Like the rest of the set it is compact and light, telecentric for even illumination into the corners, with low distortion and high resolution. It carries the Summicron-C rendering of natural, dimensional skin tones, a gentle focus roll-off and a naturalistic bokeh, colour-matched to intercut cleanly with the faster Summilux-C primes. PL mount, a 95mm front and matched gear positions keep it consistent across the set.

lenses.description: A standard-to-short-telephoto T2.0 prime in the Leitz Summicron-C set for Super 35, a classic choice for dialogue and portraiture. Like the set it is compact and light, telecentric for even illumination, with low distortion and clean high resolution. The natural, dimensional skin tones and gentle focus fall-off flatter faces, with a soft, naturalistic bokeh, colour-matched to intercut with the Summilux-C primes. PL mount, a 95mm front and matched gears keep it interchangeable across the set.

lenses.description: The widest lens in the Leitz Summilux-C set, a T1.4 ultra-wide for Super 35 that opens up tight interiors and sweeping establishing shots. Like the rest of the set it stays compact and light for its speed, with low distortion, telecentric even illumination into the corners and virtually no focus breathing. It carries the Summilux-C signature of high resolution with warm, creamy skin tones and a gentle focus roll-off, and a naturalistic rather than clinical bokeh from purely spherical optics. A titanium PL mount, shared 95mm front and matched gear positions keep it interchangeable with the other focal lengths.

lenses.description: A short-telephoto T2.2 prime in the Leitz Thalia 65 set for large format, well suited to portraits and tighter framing on 65-format and full-frame sensors. Based on Leica's S medium-format glass, it carries the Thalia character of gentle, lower-contrast rendering, warm natural skin tones and a layered focus fall-off that gives faces depth and dimensionality. The always-round 15-blade iris gives a bokeh full of character. PL and LPL swappable mounts with /i metadata, a 95mm front and matched gears keep it interchangeable across the set.

lenses.description: The widest lens in the Leitz Thalia 65 set and its newest addition, a T3.6 ultra-wide for large format that covers sensors up to the ARRI Alexa 65. Based on Leica's S medium-format glass, it carries a vintage-inspired character without the drawbacks of true vintage glass: lower contrast than the Summilux-C or Summicron-C, a gentle painterly rendering and a layered focus roll-off that pulls the subject out of the background. Skin tones are natural and warm with subtle highlight bloom, and the 15-blade iris stays round through every stop for a bokeh full of character. PL and LPL swappable mounts with /i metadata, a 95mm front and matched gears keep it consistent across the set.

lenses.description: A wide-angle T3.6 prime in the Leitz Thalia 65 set for large format, and one of the three 1:2 macro lenses in the line, so it doubles for insert, product and extreme close-up work without diopters. Based on Leica's S medium-format glass, it shares the Thalia character: lower contrast than the faster Leitz cine sets, a gentle painterly rendering, warm natural skin tones and a layered focus roll-off that adds dimensionality. The 15-blade iris stays round through every stop for a bokeh full of character. PL and LPL swappable mounts with /i metadata, a 95mm front and matched gears keep it interchangeable across the set.

lenses.description: The Thalia-T 90mm, a T2.2 short-telephoto that stands apart from the rest of the Leitz Thalia 65 set: its optical design revives the 1935 Leica Thambar, giving a dreamy, romantic, slightly hazy softness wide open that evokes old-Hollywood portraiture. Stopped down it grows progressively sharper and more modern, so a single lens spans a glamorous soft look and a cleaner one. It shares the set's large-format coverage, warm natural skin tones and the always-round 15-blade iris for a characterful bokeh. PL and LPL swappable mounts with /i metadata, a 95mm front and matched gears keep it consistent with the rest of the set.

lenses.description: The wider of the two LEITZ ZOOM full-frame cine zooms, holding a constant T2.8 across the range with no ramping, so lighting and exposure stay put from a master to a close-up. Its parfocal design holds focus through the zoom, allowing in-shot zoom pulls, with virtually no breathing or image shift and exceptionally low distortion. Colour-matched to the Leitz primes, it intercuts seamlessly with the Summilux-C, Summicron-C and Thalia sets, delivering edge-to-edge illumination and resolution on sensors up to VistaVision. A 15-blade clickless iris renders round, natural bokeh, on swappable PL or LPL mounts with /i metadata and a 114mm front.

lenses.description: The longer of the two LEITZ ZOOM full-frame cine zooms, holding a constant T2.8 across the range with no ramping, reaching from a standard view into telephoto compression without touching lighting or exposure. Its parfocal design holds focus through the zoom for clean in-shot zoom pulls, with virtually no breathing or image shift and exceptionally low distortion. Colour-matched to the Leitz primes, it intercuts seamlessly with the Summilux-C, Summicron-C and Thalia sets, with edge-to-edge illumination and resolution on sensors up to VistaVision. A 15-blade clickless iris renders round, natural bokeh, on swappable PL or LPL mounts with /i metadata; ring positions and size match its 25-75mm companion for fast swaps.

lenses.description: A boutique large-format close-focus zoom from MasterBuilt, tuned to match the vintage character of their Classic Primes: soft edges, gentle flares and a warmer, lower-contrast rendering than clinical modern glass. Parfocal and unusually close-focusing, it covers full-frame up to large-format sensors while holding a constant aperture, making it a flexible single-lens companion for handheld and gimbal work.

A longer portrait and detail prime in Meike's budget full-frame cine line, reaching for tighter framing and compression. It shares the set's consistent color rendering, soft bokeh and matched handling across the range.

The longest lens in Meike's full-frame Prime Cine set, built for portraiture, detail and compressed perspectives. It rounds out the range with the line's uniform color, soft bokeh and affordable, solidly-made construction.

The ultra-wide anchor of Meike's affordable full-frame Prime Cine set, opening up landscapes, interiors and establishing shots. Like the rest of the line it is a solid, well-built value lens with a rounded iris for soft, near-circular bokeh and color that stays consistent across the focal range.

A wide-angle prime in Meike's budget-friendly full-frame cine line, useful for environmental coverage and handheld work. It shares the set's uniform color balance, soft rounded bokeh and matched cine gearing, offering cinema-house handling at a fraction of the cost.

A wide-normal workhorse in Meike's full-frame Prime Cine set, at home on interviews, documentary and run-and-gun narrative. It carries the line's consistent color, soft bokeh and solid mechanical build at an accessible price.

The standard prime in Meike's affordable full-frame cine line, a natural choice for dialogue and everyday coverage. It renders soft, rounded bokeh with color matched to the rest of the set and the same cine-standard gearing throughout.

A short-telephoto portrait length in Meike's full-frame Prime Cine set, flattering for faces with smooth background separation. It keeps the line's uniform color and rounded bokeh, a popular value pick for interviews and close work.

The longest lens in NiSi's Athena Prime range, reaching into portraiture, detail and compression work. Larger than its siblings and not weight-matched to them, it still carries the set's clean, neutral look, soft bokeh and controlled breathing at an accessible price.

The widest lens in NiSi's Athena Prime range, this ultra-wide gives the set its reach into landscapes, architecture and tight interiors. Like the rest of the line it renders a clean, neutral full-frame image with soft bokeh, strong micro-contrast and very low chromatic aberration, bringing modern cine-house character to an accessible price point.

A wide-angle entry in NiSi's affordable Athena Prime line, well suited to environmental and establishing work. It shares the set's clean, neutral rendering, soft bokeh and controlled focus breathing, matched in size and gearing to its siblings for quick swaps on a gimbal.

A wide-normal focal length in NiSi's Athena Prime set, versatile for interiors, group work and handheld coverage. It carries the line's neutral full-frame look, soft bokeh and low chromatic aberration, with the bright maximum aperture shared across the core primes.

A classic wide-normal workhorse in NiSi's Athena Prime line, equally at home on interviews, documentary and narrative work. It renders the set's clean, neutral full-frame image with soft bokeh and strong micro-contrast, and stays matched to its siblings for consistent handling.

A natural, near-normal perspective in NiSi's Athena Prime set, a comfortable general-purpose length sitting between the wide and standard primes. It shares the line's neutral rendering, soft bokeh and minimized focus breathing at the range's accessible price.

The standard prime in NiSi's Athena Prime line, a go-to normal length for dialogue and everyday coverage. It gives the set's clean, neutral full-frame look with soft bokeh and very low chromatic aberration, matched in size and gearing to the rest of the range.

A short-telephoto portrait lens in NiSi's Athena Prime set, flattering for faces and close-ups with soft, well-separated bokeh. It keeps the line's neutral, low-aberration rendering and matched handling for fast changes on set.

lenses.description: The long-range zoom in Sigma's AF Cine Line, a full-frame 3.75x that holds a constant T3 from wide to portrait reach. Built on the optics of the 28-105mm F2.8 Art, it pairs linear-motor autofocus with a manual switch, a limited-rotation focus ring, 0.8 pitch gears and a clickless aperture ring. The 12-blade iris renders soft, round highlights, the zoom barrel is manual, and the 95mm front fits standard matte boxes. Power and lens metadata run through the E-mount contacts, making it an all-purpose zoom for documentary and run-and-gun work.

lenses.description: The first lens in Sigma's AF Cine Line, a full-frame zoom that holds a constant T2 and adds high-speed autofocus from an HLA linear motor, switchable to manual. It carries the optics of the 28-45mm F1.8 Art into a cine housing with a limited-rotation focus ring, 0.8 pitch gears and a clickless aperture ring. The 11-blade iris keeps out-of-focus highlights soft and round, and the 95mm front fits standard matte boxes. Power and lens metadata run through the E-mount contacts, which suits gimbal, documentary and solo work where autofocus is an asset.

lenses.description: The first lens in Sigma's AF Cine Line, a full-frame zoom that holds a constant T2 and adds high-speed autofocus from an HLA linear motor, switchable to manual. It carries the optics of the 28-45mm F1.8 Art into a cine housing with a limited-rotation focus ring, 0.8 pitch gears and a clickless aperture ring. The 11-blade iris keeps out-of-focus highlights soft and round, and the 95mm front fits standard matte boxes. Power and lens metadata run through the L-mount contacts, which suits gimbal, documentary and solo work where autofocus is an asset.

lenses.description: A Super 35 cine zoom in Sigma's High Speed Zoom line, covering the core wide-to-normal range at a constant T2 with no ramping. Built on the optics of Sigma's 18-35mm F1.8 Art, it resolves cleanly for 6K to 8K work with minimal flare and ghosting. The 9-blade clickless iris renders round highlights, focus travels 180 degrees on an internal focus design, and the 95mm front and shared gear positions match its 50-100mm companion for fast changes. The sealed housing is dust- and splash-resistant, and color is matched across the line for easy intercutting.

lenses.description: Sigma's full-frame cine zoom in the classic line, a short wide zoom that holds a constant T2.2 with no ramping. It shares the optics of the 24-35mm F2 Art and behaves like a variable prime, standing in for a 24mm and a 35mm in one housing, with negligible focus breathing. The 9-blade clickless iris renders round highlights, focus travels 180 degrees, and the 95mm front and standardized gears match the rest of Sigma's cine line. This EF version can be converted to E-mount by a Sigma service center.

lenses.description: The telephoto companion in Sigma's High Speed Zoom line, a Super 35 cine zoom that holds a constant T2 across 50-100mm with no ramping. Its optics come from the 50-100mm F1.8 Art and resolve for 6K to 8K capture, with the same 9-blade clickless iris for round out-of-focus highlights. The internal focus design gives 180 degrees of focus travel, and the sealed housing is dust- and splash-resistant.

lenses.description: Sony's full-frame cine-style power zoom, a parfocal constant-f/4 lens with a servo zoom rocker, OSS stabilization and independent zoom, focus and iris rings. Built for video on E-mount cinema and mirrorless bodies, it holds focus through the zoom and delivers smooth, quiet powered moves, bridging still-lens convenience and cine-zoom behaviour.

The wide end of Thypoch's ultracompact Simera-C full-frame cine set, derived from the company's Simera rangefinder-style photo primes but built around a rounded 16-blade iris for smoother, onion-ring-free bokeh. It pairs an environmental wide perspective with a bright constant aperture and a moderately high-contrast rendering, and its tiny size makes the set one of the few that fit gimbals like the DJI Ronin 4D for low-angle, over-the-shoulder and tight-interior work.

A mild wide-angle in Thypoch's ultracompact Simera-C full-frame cine line, natural for reportage-style framing and environmental coverage. It shares the set's bright constant aperture, rounded 16-blade bokeh and moderately high-contrast look in a feather-light body suited to gimbals and tight spaces.

A classic wide-normal storytelling length in Thypoch's Simera-C full-frame cine set. It carries the line's bright constant aperture, creamy 16-blade bokeh and compact, gimbal-friendly build, matched in look and handling to its siblings for quick swaps.

The normal-perspective workhorse of Thypoch's ultracompact Simera-C cine line, a neutral standard length for dialogue and coverage. It shares the set's bright constant aperture, smooth rounded bokeh and moderately high-contrast rendering in a very small full-frame body.

The short-telephoto portrait length in Thypoch's Simera-C full-frame cine set, flattering for faces and close coverage with strong subject separation. It keeps the line's bright constant aperture and creamy 16-blade bokeh in a slightly larger but still compact body, matched to the rest of the set.

lenses.description: The wide anchor of Tokina's Cinema ATX trio, a Super 35 ultra-wide zoom rehoused from Tokina's photographic wide-angle glass into an all-metal cine body. It holds a constant T2.9 across the zoom range and a parfocal design that keeps focus through the move, with low distortion and limited focus breathing. A nine-blade iris, 0.8 mod FIZ gears and roughly 300 degrees of focus rotation make it follow-focus friendly, and it is colour-matched to the 25-75 and 50-135 in the set. An affordable, compact ultra-wide cine zoom for Super 35 productions.

lenses.description: The standard mid-range zoom of the Cinema ATX trio, covering a Super 35 image circle of around 36mm that also reaches select larger sensors. It maintains a constant T2.9 with a parfocal design that holds focus through zooms, limited breathing and low distortion, colour-matched to the 11-20 and 50-135. A clickless nine-blade iris, 0.8 mod cine gears and a 95mm front make it rig- and matte-box-friendly. It is the versatile middle lens of an affordable Super 35 zoom set.

lenses.description: The telephoto member of the Cinema ATX trio and a MkII revision, extending the set into short-to-medium telephoto coverage on Super 35. It keeps the trio's constant T2.9, parfocal design, limited breathing and low distortion, colour-matched to the 11-20 and 25-75 for a consistent look. A nine-blade iris, 0.8 mod FIZ gears and matched mechanics support follow-focus work. It completes an affordable, compact wide-to-tele Super 35 zoom set.

The longest of the seven Cinema Vista primes in this set and the fast short-telephoto of the line, notable as a T1.5 lens at this focal length. It brings strong subject isolation and compression with the series' sharp, high-resolution, neutral rendering across full-frame and larger sensors. Smooth bokeh from the nine-blade iris, near-zero breathing and matched gearing keep it interchangeable with the shorter primes. It holds the clean Vista baseline look.

The widest prime in the Cinema Vista set and a genuine engineering feat: a fast T1.5 ultra-wide that still throws an image circle large enough to cover full-frame, VistaVision and larger sensors. It renders with the clean, neutral, high-resolution character that defines the standard Vista line, the reference look from which the Vista-C and Vista-P variants were derived. Distortion and chromatic aberration are held low for a lens this wide, with a rounded nine-blade iris and roughly 300 degrees of focus rotation. Of the set it shows marginally more focus breathing than the longer primes, though the effect stays slight.

A wide-angle Cinema Vista that carries the line's purpose-built, neutral rendering into interiors and landscapes. It resolves cleanly across a full-frame and VistaVision image circle with low distortion and well-controlled chromatic aberration, colour-matched to the rest of the set. Near-zero focus breathing, a rounded iris and a shared front diameter and gear position make it a direct swap with its neighbours. This is the clean baseline optic, not the vintage-flavoured Vista-C or the swirl-heavy Vista-P.

A wide-normal and one of the workhorse focal lengths of the Cinema Vista set, suited to establishing shots and moving work. It keeps the series' sharp, high-resolution, low-distortion rendering across large-format coverage, colour-matched for seamless cutting with the other primes. Virtually no focus breathing and identical mechanics support fast lens changes on set. It represents the clean, neutral Vista baseline behind the character variants.

A storytelling wide-normal at the heart of the Cinema Vista set, a natural choice for dialogue and coverage. Purpose-built for cinema rather than rehoused, it delivers tack-sharp resolution wide open at T1.5 with low distortion and neutral colour across full-frame and larger sensors. Near-zero breathing, a nine-blade round iris and matched gearing keep it consistent with the rest of the range. It is the clean reference look behind the Vista-C and Vista-P character variants.

The normal lens of the Cinema Vista set and a natural anchor for the range. It pairs a sharp, high-resolution centre with even, neutral rendering across a 46.7mm image circle, holding the line's low distortion and controlled chromatic aberration. The nine-blade iris gives round, natural bokeh, focus breathing is virtually absent, and the barrel matches the set for quick swaps. This is the clean baseline Vista optic.

The classic portrait focal length of the Cinema Vista set, delivering flattering compression with the line's clean, modern rendering. Sharp and high-resolution wide open at T1.5, it isolates the subject against smooth, natural bokeh from the rounded nine-blade iris, with neutral colour and low aberration across full-frame coverage. Focus breathing is virtually absent and the mechanics match the rest of the set. It is the neutral baseline, without the flares of the Vista-C or the swirl of the Vista-P.

lenses.description: A full-frame and VistaVision wide-angle zoom that rounds out the Vista line and shares the physical design of the Cinema Vista primes, so it mixes cleanly with them: a 114mm front, roughly 300 degrees of focus rotation and a 112mm filter thread. Rehoused from Tokina's AT-X 16-28 photographic optic, the cine version adds a longer focus throw and an all-metal housing, using aspherical and super-low-dispersion glass for good colour correction and low distortion. It holds a constant T3.0 with a parfocal design that does not ramp exposure and keeps breathing and image shift low through the zoom. As one of relatively few affordable full-frame wide cine zooms, it is a practical wide companion to the Vista primes.

lenses.description: A short-telephoto full-frame prime in Tokina's Vista-C line, unusually fast for its reach at T1.5. It keeps the set's vintage character, lower contrast, gentle distortion and warm flares, alongside high resolution and near-zero focus breathing, and the nine-blade curved iris renders smooth, round highlights for strong subject isolation. Its image circle covers large-format sensors. The 114mm front, long focus throw and common gear positions keep it consistent with the rest of the set.

lenses.description: The widest lens in Tokina's Vista-C set, a full-frame ultra-wide that carries the line's deliberately vintage character at a fast T1.5. Where the standard Vista primes chase a clean, uniform look, the Vista-C redesign lowers contrast, introduces subtle spherical distortion and softens the edges, trading the usual green flares for warmer red and blue ones. Its image circle covers full frame comfortably, though at this wide focal length it does not fully reach the very largest formats such as ALEXA 65 Open Gate. The curved nine-blade iris keeps out-of-focus highlights round, and the shared 114mm front, long focus throw and common gear positions let it swap quickly with the rest of the set.
