The curved nine-blade iris keeps out-of-focus highlights round, and the shared 114mm front, long focus throw and common gear positions let it swap quickly with the rest of the set.

lenses.description: The classic portrait focal length in Tokina's Vista-C set, a full-frame prime fast at T1.5. Its vintage-tuned optics, lowered contrast and soft falloff suit close work, giving flattering skin rendering with smooth, round bokeh from the nine-blade curved iris, while resolution stays high and breathing near zero. Coverage runs beyond full frame to large formats. It shares the 114mm front, roughly 300 degrees of focus rotation and matched gear positions with the rest of the line for fast changes.

lenses.description: A telephoto portrait lens that brings stronger compression to the Vista-P line. The deliberate spherical character and reduced contrast wrap the out-of-focus areas around a crisp centre, giving close and medium portraits a flattering, organic separation. It holds the series standard of minimal breathing, a matched iris and a long, even focus feel for repeatable marks. The stylised fall-off is most pronounced on full-frame and larger sensors.

lenses.description: A long portrait telephoto in the Vista-P set, built for pronounced subject isolation and compression. The Portrait re-engineering keeps a sharp, high-resolution centre while the background falls off with the line's soft, swirling, lower-contrast rendering. Near-zero breathing and matched mechanics make it interchangeable with the rest of the set on the day. Its character is strongest wide open across full-frame coverage.

lenses.description: The longest lens in the Vista-P set and the one telephoto that opens a touch slower than the rest of the primes, developed to give large-format work a fast super-telephoto reach. It delivers dramatic compression and subject isolation with a sharp centre and the line's soft, spherically-influenced, lower-contrast fall-off in the background. Internal focusing keeps its length fixed and breathing near zero, and it carries the same matched front and gearing as the shorter primes despite its size. The character suits stylised portrait and specialty telephoto work on full-frame and larger sensors.

lenses.description: The widest Vista-P prime, a factory re-engineering of the standard Vista in which elements are repositioned throughout the design to introduce deliberate spherical aberration and reduced contrast. At this wide angle the Petzval-like swirl and field character sit most visibly toward the edges of the full-frame image, while the centre keeps the crisp, high-resolution rendering of the base Vista optics. It retains the series baseline of near-zero focus breathing, a rounded iris and a matched front for quick swaps. The stylised edge effect is strongest across full-frame coverage and relaxes on Super 35.

lenses.description: A wide-angle Vista-P that carries the same factory modification of the Vista base design, trading a measure of contrast and correction for character. The repositioned elements bring a gentle spherical fall-off and swirl to the outer frame while the centre stays sharp, giving wide interiors and landscapes an organic, slightly vintage feel. Focus breathing is effectively absent and the barrel matches the rest of the set for gearing and front diameter. On Super 35 the edge character tightens up.

lenses.description: A wide-normal in the Vista-P line and one of the most-used focal lengths for establishing and moving work. The lens keeps the sharp Vista centre but layers in the deliberate lower contrast and edge swirl that define the Portrait variant, sitting between the ultra-wides and the normals in how pronounced that character reads. Near-zero breathing and a matched barrel keep it interchangeable with the rest of the set. The effect eases on smaller sensors.

lenses.description: A wide-normal in the Vista-P line and one of the most-used focal lengths for establishing and moving work. The lens keeps the sharp Vista centre but layers in the deliberate lower contrast and edge swirl that define the Portrait variant, sitting between the ultra-wides and the normals in how pronounced that character reads. Near-zero breathing and a matched barrel keep it interchangeable with the rest of the set. The effect eases on smaller sensors.

lenses.description: A slightly tighter wide-normal that fills the gap between the 25 and 35 in the Vista-P set. It shares the family's re-engineered Vista optics: a clean, high-resolution centre with a softer, spherically-influenced edge and the restrained contrast of the Portrait line. The lens holds the Vista mechanical standard, with minimal breathing and matched gearing for fast changes. Its character is present but measured at this focal length.

lenses.description: A storytelling wide-normal and one of the core focal lengths of the Vista-P set. The Portrait re-engineering gives it a sharp centre with the line's signature swirl and gentle contrast roll-off toward the edges, an organic look that stays controlled enough for dialogue and coverage. Full-frame and larger coverage, near-zero breathing and a matched front keep it consistent with its neighbours. On Super 35 the frame reads cleaner.

lenses.description: A near-normal Vista-P that renders a natural, close-to-human perspective with the line's added character. The repositioned elements introduce the deliberate spherical fall-off and lower contrast that separate the Portrait variant from the clean standard Vista, while the centre remains crisp and high-resolution. It matches the set for front diameter, gearing, iris and focus feel, and shows effectively no breathing. The stylised look is most evident wide open on full-frame.

lenses.description: The normal lens of the Vista-P set and a natural anchor for the line's Portrait intent. Its centre is sharp and high-resolution while the repositioned Vista elements wrap the transition zones and background in a soft, swirling fall-off with restrained contrast. The result is a flattering, characterful rendering that still resolves cleanly, with near-zero breathing and matched mechanics across the set. The swirl reads strongest on full-frame and calms on Super 35.

The Portrait re-engineering gives it a clean centre with a gentle spherical fall-off and swirl behind the subject, lending close portraits an organic, slightly vintage separation. It shares the family's near-zero breathing, matched iris and front for quick changes. Character increases toward the edges of the full-frame image.

lenses.description: The classic portrait focal length of the Vista-P set, where the line's intent is most at home. A sharp, high-resolution centre isolates the subject while the repositioned Vista elements render the surrounding fall-off with a soft, swirling, lower-contrast character. Near-zero focus breathing keeps the frame stable through slow pulls, and the barrel matches the rest of the set for gearing and front size. The effect is at its most expressive wide open on full-frame.

lenses.description: The classic portrait focal length of the Vista-P set, where the line's intent is most at home. A sharp, high-resolution centre isolates the subject while the repositioned Vista elements render the surrounding fall-off with a soft, swirling, lower-contrast character. Near-zero focus breathing keeps the frame stable through slow pulls, and the barrel matches the rest of the set for gearing and front size. The effect is at its most expressive wide open on full-frame.

lenses.description: A statement full-frame and large-format cine zoom from Viltrox, holding a constant aperture and parfocal focus across an extreme wide-to-long-telephoto range without ramping or focus shift. Its enormous unified white barrel is built to resist thermal expansion, and it targets wildlife, sports and other long-reach work where a single lens must cover a vast focal span. Filters attach magnetically and it is designed around standard matte-box workflows.

It keeps the line's metal build, common front diameter and matched colour so it cuts cleanly against the faster primes, with internal focus holding breathing low on long pulls. lenses.description: The ultra-wide anchor of Samyang's original XEEN set, the brand's first metal-housed full-frame cine primes and a value-priced route into a colour-matched cine kit. At a slower T3.1 it takes in a sweeping field behind a bulbous front element, so it works with a matte box rather than screw-in filters. Internal focus keeps breathing minimal and the rendering is neutral and cleanly corrected, matched to the rest of the XEEN line for intercutting. A common wide establishing lens on indie features and documentary.

It shares the CP.2 interchangeable mount (PL, EF, F, E, MFT) for flexible use across cine and DSLR cameras, with a 14-blade iris for round bokeh well suited to tight portraits and isolation. The T* coating suppresses flare, and focus and iris are manual on a long throw.

lenses.description: The widest lens in Zeiss's Compact Prime CP.2 line, a full-frame cine prime at T2.9. Like the rest of the CP.2 set it uses an interchangeable mount that swaps between PL, EF, F, E and MFT, so a single lens serves both cine and stills bodies without adapters. A 14-blade iris keeps out-of-focus highlights round across the aperture range, and the T* coating with internal light traps holds flare down. Focus and iris are manual, on a long focus rotation.

lenses.description: The widest lens in Zeiss's CP.3 line, a compact full-frame prime at T2.9. The CP.3 set is the third-generation Compact Prime, smaller and lighter than the CP.2 while still covering full frame, with a 14-blade iris that keeps out-of-focus highlights round through the T-stop range. All ten share a 95mm front and matched gear positions, and the interchangeable mount swaps between PL, EF, F, E and MFT, with an eXtended Data option that transmits lens metadata over Cooke /i and Zeiss datasets. Focus is manual on a smooth 300-degree rotation.

lenses.description: The wide zoom in Zeiss's Compact Zoom CZ.2 line, a full-frame cine zoom holding a constant T2.9 across a 15-30mm range. Like the rest of the CZ.2 set it is parfocal, keeping focus through the zoom, and covers full frame for large-sensor cameras. It uses Zeiss's interchangeable mount so a single lens serves cine and DSLR bodies, with the clean Zeiss rendering and eXtended Data metadata. A useful wide-to-normal range for run-and-gun and documentary work.

lenses.description: Zeiss's Lightweight Zoom LWZ.2, a compact Super 35 cine zoom covering 15.5-45mm at a constant T2.6. Built light for documentary, ENG and gimbal work, it is parfocal, holding focus through the zoom, on a PL mount. It offers a useful wide-to-normal range with the Zeiss rendering in a package sized for run-and-gun shooting.

lenses.description: Zeiss's Lightweight Zoom LWZ.3, a compact Super 35 cine zoom with a wide 21-100mm range, nearly 5x. Its aperture ramps across the zoom, from T2.9 at the wide end to T3.9 at full telephoto, and it is parfocal, holding focus through the range on a PL mount. Light enough for documentary, ENG and gimbal setups, it trades a little speed at the long end for reach and portability.

lenses.description: The widest lens in Zeiss's Nano Prime line, a compact full-frame cine prime that stays fast at T1.5 even at the wide end. The Nano Primes are Zeiss's lightweight mirrorless set, native to Sony E and user-swappable to RF, Z or L through the ZEISS interchangeable mount system, small enough for gimbals and drones. A 12-blade iris gives an elegant, circular bokeh, with the gentle sharpness Zeiss tunes to sit alongside the Supreme Primes as a B or C camera. All six share a 95mm front, an 86mm filter thread and eXtended Data metadata.

lenses.description: A wide-normal prime in Zeiss's Panoptes 65 line, the company's dedicated large-format set for 65mm sensors. Its 59.9mm image circle covers cameras like the ARRI Alexa 265 and Blackmagic URSA Cine 17K 65 on an LPL mount, at a uniform T2.2 shared across the set. Zeiss tunes the Panoptes for a clean, precise look rather than overt character: natural color, forgiving skin tones, gentle focus fall-off and a smooth bokeh, with distortion and chromatic aberration held low. It shares a 95mm front, matched ring positions and eXtended Data support with the rest of the line for VFX and virtual-production workflows.

lenses.description: An ultra-wide full-frame prime in Zeiss's Supreme Prime line, fast at T1.5. Like the rest of the set it uses an internal focus design for minimal breathing and a non-rotating front, with a 16-blade iris that keeps out-of-focus highlights round. Color and handling are matched across the Supreme lenses, with shared gear positions and metadata over Cooke /i and Zeiss eXtended Data. The wide optics carry a larger front than the mid range, and the mount is a field-interchangeable PL.

lenses.description: The longest lens in Zeiss's Supreme Prime line, a full-frame telephoto prime at T2.2, a touch slower than the T1.5 core of the set. It keeps the line's internal focus design for minimal breathing and a non-rotating front, with the same 16-blade iris for round bokeh. Color and rendering stay matched to the other Supremes, and the focus and iris gears sit in the standard positions for fast changes. The mount is field-interchangeable and ships in PL.

lenses.description: The long end of Zeiss's Supreme Prime Radiance line, a full-frame telephoto prime still fast at T1.5. Like the rest of the Radiance set it is a Supreme Prime with the T* blue coating for controlled, planned flares and a slightly warmer look, while retaining the Supreme optics: 46.3mm full-frame coverage, a 16-blade iris for smooth bokeh, a 300-degree focus rotation and eXtended Data. With the 18mm it carries the line's larger 114mm front rather than the 95mm used across the middle of the range, and it suits large-format portraiture and isolation.

lenses.description: The wide end of Zeiss's Supreme Prime Radiance line, a full-frame cine prime at a fast T1.5. The Radiance lenses are the Supreme Primes rebuilt with the T* blue coating, which lets a cinematographer create controlled, repeatable flares under directed light while keeping contrast and transmission intact, with a slightly warmer rendering than the standard Supremes. It keeps the Supreme optics otherwise: 46.3mm full-frame coverage, a 16-blade iris for smooth bokeh, a 300-degree focus rotation and eXtended Data metadata. Along with the 135mm it uses a larger 114mm front, the two exceptions to the line's 95mm standard.

lenses.description: The wide zoom in Zeiss's Supreme Zoom Radiance line, a full-frame parfocal cine zoom at a constant T2.9 across 15-30mm. These are the only zooms built to match the Supreme Prime Radiance look: the T* blue coating produces controlled, reproducible blue flares under directed light and a slightly warmer rendition, while normal lighting renders cleanly. The flare shape even shifts as you zoom, and the lens holds focus through the range. It carries the Radiance line's 114mm front, matched gear positions and eXtended Data metadata.

lenses.description: The telephoto zoom in Zeiss's Supreme Zoom Radiance line, a full-frame parfocal cine zoom at a constant T2.9 across 70-200mm. It shares the T* blue coating for controlled blue flares and a warmer rendition, rendering cleanly under normal light, and holds focus through the zoom. Matched to the Supreme Prime Radiance and Supreme Primes in gear position and look, it carries the 114mm front and eXtended Data, and is the heaviest of the three Radiance zooms.
